idea.hook: WHAT IF Earth orbited a NEUTRON STAR? ✨
facts: It's ~20km wide, but packs 1.4x the Sun's mass.
Surface gravity is 200 BILLION times Earth's, distorting spacetime itself.
It spins up to 700 times/second, blasting intense X-rays & gamma rays.
idea.payoff: Earth would be instantly vaporized. Your 'day' would be a terrifying, ultra-fast cosmic blur... for the fraction of a second you existed.
idea.title: Orbiting a NEUTRON STAR: A Cosmic Nightmare! 😵‍💫🌌

scenes.text: WHAT IF Earth orbited a NEUTRON STAR? ✨
scenes.visual: A serene view of Earth rotating in space. A tiny, intensely bright star (neutron star) is visible in the distance, slowly pulsating.

Zoom in on the neutron star. An outline of Earth appears next to it for scale, showing the neutron star as tiny compared to Earth's outline, then text indicating its immense mass appears next to the neutron star.

scenes.text: Surface gravity: 200 BILLION x Earth's. Distorts spacetime!
scenes.visual: The neutron star is shown with a strong gravitational lensing effect around it, severely bending light from background stars or a grid pattern.
The neutron star spins incredibly fast, emitting powerful, cone-shaped beams of X-rays and gamma rays into space, sweeping across the screen.

scenes.text: Earth: INSTANTLY VAPORIZED. A terrifying, ultra-fast blur... for a fraction of a second.
scenes.visual: Earth rapidly approaches the violently spinning neutron star. It distorts, stretches, rips apart, and then explodes into a flash of cosmic dust and energy, leaving only the neutron star pulsing intensely.
